title: Utility classes should avoid having public constructors
description: Utility classes should not have public constructorsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmelldesignWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?Whenever there are portions of code that are duplicated and do not depend on the state of their container class, they can be centralized inside a "utility class". A utility class is a class that only has static members, hence it should not be instantiated.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects non-static classes containing only static members but with implicit public constructor

checks.message: Utility class should be static or have a private constructor

logic.triggering_conditions: Public non-static class with static methods but no private constructor
logic.exclusion_conditions: Static classes, classes with private constructor, classes with instance members
logic.reasoning: Utility classes with only static members should either be declared as static classes or have a private constructor to prevent instantiation. Instantiating such classes wastes memory and indicates design confusion.

should_trigger: public class StringUtils { public static string Reverse(string s) { } }
public class MathHelper { public static int Add(int a, int b) { } }
should_not_trigger: public class MathUtils { private MathUtils() { } public static int Add(int a, int b) { } }
public class Service { public void DoWork() { } }
